description: Add async progress callback system to the billfox pipeline so consumers can receive typed events at each stage boundary (loading, preprocessing, extracting, parsing, storing)

userStories.title: Create progress event types
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want typed progress events so I can pattern-match on stage and status in my callback.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new file src/billfox/_progress.py
Add Stage enum with values: LOADING, PREPROCESSING, EXTRACTING, PARSING, STORING
Add Status enum with values: STARTED, COMPLETED, FAILED
Add frozen ProgressEvent dataclass with fields: stage (Stage), status (Status), message (str | None = None), metadata (dict[str, object] | None = None)
Add ProgressCallback type alias: Callable[[ProgressEvent], Awaitable[None]]
Export Stage, Status, ProgressEvent, ProgressCallback from billfox/__init__.py and add to __all__
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use from __future__ import annotations. Follow existing frozen dataclass pattern from _types.py.

userStories.title: Add progress callback to Pipeline.run() with error handling
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want to pass an on_progress callback to Pipeline so I receive STARTED, COMPLETED, and FAILED events during run() execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline dataclass gets on_progress: ProgressCallback | None = None field
Add private async _emit helper method that no-ops when on_progress is None
run() emits STARTED before and COMPLETED after each stage: LOADING, PREPROCESSING, EXTRACTING, PARSING, STORING
PREPROCESSING events only emitted when self.preprocessors is non-empty
STORING events only emitted when self.store is not None and document_id is not None
PREPROCESSING STARTED emits message=type(p).__name__ for each preprocessor in the chain
EXTRACTING COMPLETED includes metadata={'pages': len(result.pages)}
Each stage is wrapped in try/except: on exception, emit ProgressEvent(stage, Status.FAILED, message=str(error)) then re-raise the original exception
Pipeline with no on_progress behaves identically to before (zero overhead)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Import Stage, Status, ProgressEvent, ProgressCallback from billfox._progress. The _emit helper should early-return if on_progress is None.

userStories.title: Add progress callback to Pipeline.extract_only()
userStories.description: As a library consumer, I want progress events when using extract_only() so CLI extract commands can show progress too.
acceptanceCriteria: extract_only() emits LOADING STARTED/COMPLETED events around source.load()
extract_only() emits PREPROCESSING STARTED/COMPLETED events around _preprocess() when preprocessors exist
extract_only() emits EXTRACTING STARTED/COMPLETED events around extractor.extract()
No PARSING or STORING events are emitted in extract_only()
Each stage in extract_only() is wrapped in try/except with FAILED event emission, same as run()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern established in run() by US-002. Reuse the _emit helper.

userStories.title: Integrate progress into CLI extract and parse commands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to see progress output when running billfox extract and billfox parse so I know what's happening.
acceptanceCriteria: extract command creates an async progress callback that prints stage status using rich Console(stderr=True)
parse command passes an async progress callback to Pipeline(on_progress=...) that prints stage status using rich Console(stderr=True)
Progress shows stage name on STARTED, 'done' on COMPLETED, error message on FAILED
Progress output goes to stderr so stdout remains clean for piping
Progress output is suppressed when stdout is not a TTY (check sys.stdout.isatty())
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The extract command currently runs stages manually (not via Pipeline), so create a local async callback and call it around each stage. The parse command already uses Pipeline, so pass on_progress to it. Use rich.console.Console(stderr=True) for output.

userStories.title: Test progress event system
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive tests for the progress system so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new file tests/test_progress.py
Test: pipeline with no on_progress runs normally without errors
Test: run() emits correct sequence of STARTED/COMPLETED events for all stages (LOADING, PREPROCESSING, EXTRACTING, PARSING, STORING)
Test: extract_only() emits only LOADING, PREPROCESSING, EXTRACTING events (no PARSING/STORING)
Test: FAILED event emitted with error message string when a stage raises an exception
Test: PREPROCESSING events are skipped when no preprocessors are configured
Test: STORING events are skipped when no store is configured
Test: PREPROCESSING STARTED event includes preprocessor class name in message field
Test: EXTRACTING COMPLETED event includes page count in metadata dict
All tests pass
userStories.notes: Use mock/fake implementations of the protocols (DocumentSource, Extractor, Parser, Preprocessor). Follow existing test patterns in the tests/ directory. Use pytest-asyncio for async tests.

userStories.title: DocumentBackup protocol and BackupResult type
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a DocumentBackup protocol so that backup implementations are swappable like every other billfox component.
acceptanceCriteria: BackupResult frozen dataclass in _types.py with fields: uri (str, remote location), provider (str, e.g. 'google_drive'), metadata (dict[str, str])
DocumentBackup protocol in backup/_base.py with method: async def backup(self, document: Document) -> BackupResult
Protocol is @runtime_checkable
backup/__init__.py exports DocumentBackup and BackupResult
Export BackupResult from billfox/__init__.py and add to __all__
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact same pattern as DocumentSource in source/_base.py and DocumentStore in store/_base.py. Use from __future__ import annotations. BackupResult goes in _types.py alongside Document, ExtractionResult, SearchResult.

userStories.title: Google Drive OAuth flow and credentials management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to authorize billfox to access my Google Drive via browser so that my documents can be backed up to my personal Drive.
acceptanceCriteria: GoogleDriveAuth class in backup/google_drive/auth.py handles the full OAuth flow
client_id and client_secret read from env vars GOOGLE_DRIVE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_DRIVE_CLIENT_SECRET
Scopes: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file (access only to files created by the app)
OAuth flow starts a temporary local HTTP server to receive the callback
On success, credentials (access_token, refresh_token, token_uri, client_id, client_secret, expires_at) saved to ~/.billfox/credentials/google_drive.json
Credentials file created with 0600 permissions (user-only read/write)
load_credentials() function reads from the credentials file and returns a google.oauth2.credentials.Credentials object
Automatic token refresh when access token is expired (using refresh_token)
Clear error message if credentials file is missing: Run 'billfox auth google-drive' to authorize
Dependencies google-api-python-client, google-auth-httplib2, google-auth-oauthlib added as 'google-drive' optional extra in pyproject.toml
Lazy import of google dependencies with clear error: pip install billfox[google-drive]
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference implementation: ../billfox-app/api/src/integrations/google_drive/oauth.py. Port the GoogleDriveOAuthClient pattern but adapt for CLI use (local HTTP server callback instead of web redirect). Use google_auth_oauthlib.flow.InstalledAppFlow for the local server flow. Follow the lazy import pattern used in extract/mistral.py and embed/openai.py.

userStories.title: GoogleDriveBackup implementation
userStories.description: As a user, I want my original documents automatically uploaded to Google Drive in a date-based folder structure after pipeline processing.
acceptanceCriteria: GoogleDriveBackup class in backup/google_drive/client.py implements DocumentBackup protocol
Constructor accepts optional root_folder_name: str (default 'BillFox') and optional credentials_path: str (default ~/.billfox/credentials/google_drive.json)
On first backup, creates root folder BillFox in Drive root (or finds existing one)
Folder structure: BillFox/YYYY/MM/DD/ based on current date (UTC)
Uses ensure_folder_path pattern to create nested folders, reusing existing ones
Uploads original document bytes with original filename extracted from document.source_uri
If file with same name exists in target folder, updates it instead of creating duplicate
Returns BackupResult with uri set to the Google Drive webViewLink, provider set to 'google_drive'
Google Drive API calls wrapped in asyncio.to_thread() to avoid blocking the event loop
Lazy import of google dependencies with clear error: pip install billfox[google-drive]
backup/google_drive/__init__.py exports GoogleDriveBackup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference implementation: ../billfox-app/api/src/integrations/google_drive/client.py. Port the GoogleDriveClient pattern (create_folder, find_folder, ensure_folder_path, upload_file, find_file, update_file). The Drive API client is synchronous so all calls must be wrapped in asyncio.to_thread(). Extract filename from document.source_uri using os.path.basename or PurePath.

userStories.title: Pipeline backup integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pass a backup instance to Pipeline so originals are automatically backed up after all processing completes.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline dataclass gains optional field: backup: DocumentBackup | None = None
Pipeline.run() calls backup.backup(document) after all steps complete (after parse and store)
Pipeline.extract_only() also triggers backup if configured
Backup failure does not prevent the pipeline from completing — log a warning via logging.getLogger(__name__) and continue
Pipeline.run() return type unchanged
Pipeline with no backup behaves identically to before (zero overhead)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Import DocumentBackup from billfox.backup._base. Add the backup field after store in the dataclass. Wrap backup call in try/except, log warning on failure, never re-raise. If on_progress from US-002 exists, backup should happen after all progress events.

userStories.title: CLI auth command for Google Drive
userStories.description: As a user, I want a 'billfox auth google-drive' CLI command to set up Google Drive authorization.
acceptanceCriteria: New auth command group in CLI (cli/auth.py)
'billfox auth google-drive' triggers the OAuth browser flow from US-016
Prints success message with the Google account email after authorization
If already authorized, asks for confirmation before re-authorizing
'billfox auth status' shows which integrations are authorized (checks for credentials file existence)
Auth command group registered in cli/main.py
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing CLI patterns in cli/extract.py and cli/parse.py. Use typer for the command group. Use rich Console(stderr=True) for output. Lazy import GoogleDriveAuth to avoid requiring google deps when not using backup.

userStories.title: CLI backup command for manual file uploads
userStories.description: As a user, I want a 'billfox backup <file>' command to manually back up files to Google Drive without running the full pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: 'billfox backup <file>' uploads the specified file to Google Drive
Uses the same date-based folder structure (BillFox/YYYY/MM/DD/)
Accepts multiple files: billfox backup file1.jpg file2.pdf
Prints the Google Drive link for each uploaded file
Clear error if not authorized: Run 'billfox auth google-drive' to authorize
Backup command registered in cli/main.py
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create cli/backup.py. Read file bytes and construct a Document object using LocalFileSource or directly, then pass to GoogleDriveBackup.backup(). Use rich Console(stderr=True) for status output, print Drive links to stdout.

userStories.title: Tests for backup protocol and Google Drive integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want tests covering the backup protocol, Google Drive client, and pipeline integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Unit test: GoogleDriveBackup satisfies DocumentBackup protocol (isinstance check)
Unit test: BackupResult dataclass is frozen and fields are correct
Unit test: Pipeline with backup calls backup.backup() after parse and store complete
Unit test: Pipeline continues when backup raises an exception (warning logged)
Unit test: Pipeline without backup works unchanged (no regression)
Unit test: GoogleDriveBackup constructs correct folder path for a given date
Unit test: File deduplication — update instead of create when file exists
Unit test: Credentials loading and missing-credentials error message
Mock all Google API calls (no real Drive access in unit tests)
All tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create tests/test_backup.py. Use unittest.mock.AsyncMock and unittest.mock.patch to mock Google API calls. Follow existing test patterns in tests/test_pipeline.py for pipeline integration tests. Use pytest-asyncio for async tests.
